data.title: Star Diamond CEO Ewan Mason to retire
data.description:
• Star Diamond (TSX:DIAM:CA) on Monday reported that Chair, President and CEO, Ewan Mason, has notified the board of his intention to retire on November 20.
  • Mason has served 7 years on the board and nearly 3 years as President and CEO of the company.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Humanoid Global appoints Shahab Samimi as CEO
data.description:
• Humanoid Global (OTCPK:RBOHF) announced on Monday that it has appointed Shahab Samimi as the chief executive officer, effective today.
  • Samimi has also served as a board member at Hypercharge Networks and is currently a Senior Product Manager leading enterprise software initiatives at a major Fortune 500 retail company. 
  • The Company also announces that pursuant to its stock option and RSU plans, it has granted 500,000 restricted share units and 400,000 incentive stock options to directors and officers of the company.
  • Each option is exercisable to purchase one common share of the company at an exercise price of $0.60 for a period of 5 years from the date of the grant.
  • The options will vest immediately. Each RSU entitles the recipient to receive one Share of the company on vesting. The RSUs will vest immediately and expire on August 25, 2028, said the company.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: OpenAI President, A16Z, others to invest more than $100M in PAC to defend AI: report
data.description:
• Some prominent names in Silicon Valley are investing more than $100M into a political action committee to defend artificial intelligence amongst harsh regulations, The Wall Street Journal reported. 
  • OpenAI President Greg Brockman and venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz are amongst those investing in the Leading the Future super PAC, the news outlet added, citing conversations with the group.
  • Maker of the popular ChatGPT chatbot, OpenAI is backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) and others.
  • “There is a vast force out there that’s looking to slow down AI deployment, prevent the American worker from benefiting from the U.S. leading in global innovation and job creation and erect a patchwork of regulation,” Josh Vlasto and Zac Moffatt, the group’s leaders, said in a statement obtained by the news outlet. “This is the ecosystem that is going to be the counterforce going into next year.”
  • The PAC will begin its work in New York, California, Illinois and Ohio, four states that are seen as “key” AI policy battlegrounds, The Journal added. It will also support Democrats and Republicans, with the campaign slated to start later this year.
  • Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Analysts dig into the impact of McDonald's cutting prices on combo meals
data.description:
McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) rocked the fast-food sector with reports last week that the cost of 8 combo meals will be set at 15% below the sum of the individual items' prices. The new combo meal pricing will take effect in early September.

Weighing in on the restaurant sector development, UBS expects a bigger impact from reports that McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) is bringing back Extra Value Meals at $5 for breakfast and $8 for the Big Mac meals in September and Chicken McNuggets meals in November meals. "We anticipate national marketing support for value plans will drive better value perceptions, while financial support for franchisees likely eases any profitability burden for owner-operators," noted analyst Dennis Geiger. "While MCD has not officially announced or commented on the reports, our initial franchisee discussions highlight an overwhelmingly positive response to the new plans, with a view that this will help reset everyday value in the U.S." he added.

Looking ahead, McDonald's (MCD) new pricing architecture and upcoming value bundles are seen by Geiger and his team as likely to support market share gains over time while placing some increased value pressure on fast-food peers.

However, while UBS expects value activity and intensity could further increase across the restaurant segment as a result of the McDonald's (MCD) value push, the firm believes more significant investor concerns that the rest of the segment will meaningfully underperform with margins incrementally pressured are moderately overblown.

Geiger noted that Taco Bell (NYSE:QSR) is already seeing success with its $5, $7, and $9 Luxe Boxes. Burger King (NYSE:QSR) is seen as remaining well positioned for continued momentum and market share gains due to benefits from current initiatives. UBS expects Wendy's (NASDAQ:WEN) could lean in and increase focus on menu value following the McDonald's (MCD) development.

data.title: OmniAb to raise $30M in private stock placement
data.description:
OmniAb (NASDAQ:OABI) has entered into a definitive agreement for a private placement of its common stock to raise approximately $30 million in gross proceeds. The company will issue and sell a total of 21,254,106 shares at a price of $1.40 per share.

The company expects to use the net proceeds for general corporate purposes, with the deal anticipated to close around August 26, 2025.

The financing included top-tier new and existing investors, as well as officer and director participation, the company noted.

OABI stock were down 10.81% in premarket trade on Monday.

data.title: Eltek receives purchase orders totaling $2.4 million
data.description:
• Eltek (NASDAQ:ELTK) announced on Monday that it received orders totaling $2.4 million from a defense customer.
  • The products are to be delivered mainly throughout 2026 and 2027.
  • "The customer is a leading Israeli defense company, recognized as one of the foremost companies in the country's defense sector. The order relates to products that have previously been supplied to this customer. The PCBs being delivered are critical components in the customer's systems, requiring advanced technological capabilities," commented Eli Yaffe, CEO of Eltek.
  • ELTK +0.59% after hours to $10.16.
  • Source: Press Release.

data.title: Iren doubles NVIDIA GPU fleet
data.description:
Iren (NASDAQ:IREN) said on Monday that it has doubled its NVIDIA GPU (Graphics Processing Unit) fleet, and has secured financing for a prior purchase of NVIDIA Blackwell B200 and B300 GPUs.

The bitcoin mining data centers operator has procured an additional 4.2K NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs for ~$193M, bringing its total GPU fleet to ~8.5K NVIDIA GPUs.

Total installed mining capacity is expected to remain at ~50 EH/s.

Additionally, the company announced a $102M financing, structured as a 36-month lease for 100% of the purchase price of the GPUs.

The lease payments are calculated using a high single-digit interest rate.

"Our expanded Blackwell capacity positions IREN to capture strong demand and drive the next phase of our AI Cloud revenue growth," said Co-CEO Daniel Roberts.

Shares were -1.17% pre-market to $21.18.

data.title: Driven Brands names Mo Khalid as COO
data.description:
• Driven Brands (NASDAQ:DRVN) has appointed Mo Khalid as executive vice president and chief operating officer.
  • Khalid currently serves as executive vice president and president of Take 5. He re-joined Driven Brands in 2023 as group president of the maintenance segment, which at the time included Take 5 Oil Change and Meineke.
  • Tim Austin, the current chief operating officer of Take 5, will take over as president, succeeding Khalid.

data.title: U.S. reportedly confirms first human screwworm case
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) said on Sunday it has detected the first human case in the country of New World screwworm, a flesh-eating parasite, linked to travel from an outbreak-affected nation.

The case, confirmed by the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention on Aug. 4, involved a patient who had recently returned from El Salvador, HHS spokesperson Andrew G. Nixon told Reuters in an email.

The investigation was conducted in coordination with the Maryland Department of Health. Nixon noted that "The risk to public health in the United States from this introduction is very low.”

Screwworms can be devastating in cattle and wildlife, and, though human cases are rare, infestations in either animals or people can be fatal. Treatment is arduous, requiring the removal of hundreds of larvae and thorough wound disinfection. However, infestations are generally survivable if treated promptly.

The government’s confirmation comes just over a week after U.S. Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins traveled to Texas to announce plans for a new sterile-fly production facility to help combat the pest.

The USDA estimates that a screwworm outbreak could cost Texas—the nation’s largest cattle-producing state—about $1.8 billion in livestock losses, labor costs, and medication expenses.

The presence of screwworms can likely rattle beef and livestock markets. Adding to industry anxiety, an executive at the Beef Alliance emailed about two dozen individuals in the livestock and beef sectors last week, stating that the CDC had confirmed a human screwworm case in Maryland in a traveler from Guatemala, a source told Reuters.

Conflicting reports from industry sources and the U.S. government about the human case are likely to deepen uncertainty in a sector already on edge, as livestock traders and beef producers worry about potential cattle infections amid record-high prices and the smallest U.S. herd in nearly seven decades.

Screwworms have been advancing north through Mexico from Central America since 2023 and remain endemic in Cuba, Haiti, the Dominican Republic and several South American nations, according to the USDA.

Mexico reported a fresh case in July in Ixhuatlán de Madero, Veracruz—about 370 miles (595 km) south of the U.S. border—prompting the USDA to immediately shut livestock trade through southern ports of entry. The agency had previously halted imports in November and May as part of efforts to prevent the pest from entering the U.S.

Retailers with imported beef in supply chains: Walmart (WMT), Kroger (KR), Albertsons Companies (ACI) and Costco Wholesale (COST).

data.title: Avino acquires full ownership of La Preciosa silver project in Mexico in $22M deal
data.description:
Avino Silver & Gold Mines (NYSE:ASM) said Monday it acquired 100% ownership of its La Preciosa project in Mexico from Deterra Royalties for $22M, including a $13.25M upfront payment followed by an $8.75M payment deferred for one year.

La Preciosa is one of the largest undeveloped silver resources in Mexico, and is located adjacent to Avino's (NYSE:ASM) existing mine and infrastructure, the company said.

Avino (ASM) said it has made excellent progress advancing La Preciosa, with first production expected by year-end 2025 and significant production growth expected in the following years which will see La Preciosa become a cornerstone asset for the company.

"By eliminating the royalty burden immediately prior to commencing production, we believe we will generate meaningful returns on our investment by lowering La Preciosa's operating cost profile, and ensuring that as much of La Preciosa's value remains with the operator and its stakeholders," President and CEO David Wolfin said.
